name: ddd-workshop
description: DDD pipeline with two paired skills. (1) domain-classroom — teaches general industry patterns of an unfamiliar domain in lecture mode grounded in cognitive-science 8 principles (Cognitive Load, Practice Testing, distributed practice, elaborative interrogation, self-explanation/Feynman, schema connection, dual coding, desirable difficulty). AI plays virtual domain expert (teacher), user is learner. Menu-driven priority bundles → phase deep-dive → baseline 3 Q&A with skip/continue. Laws/regulations integrated as study material. AI judges whether to ask for input materials based on domain familiarity. Output: docs/learning-notes/{domain}-classroom.md. (2) bigpicture — 1:1 analytic tool for Big Picture EventStorming. Maps general patterns from domain-classroom + client materials (RFP, requirements, schemas) into the canonical Big Picture artifact (time-ordered events, phases, actors, external systems, hotspots, pivots). No personas, no narrative, no chapters — phase-by-phase analytic accumulation. Domain-event 4-criteria gate (domain-expert relevance, business state change, legal significance, downstream trigger) prevents UI/telemetry/technical event contamination. Korean natural-language + Code Identifier dual notation. Hotspots tagged with ID, where-answered, confidence. Output: docs/eventstorming.md (single file, auto-splits to folder when later stages join). Initial/Update/Cycle modes — repeatable after code is written. UL, BC, Aggregate are explicitly excluded as they belong to later stages (Process Modeling, Strategic Design, Software Design — future skills). Together: domain-classroom for general patterns first, then bigpicture for client-specific overlay. Either runs standalone.
